title: placus indicus
side_effects: Placus indicus can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In rare cases, it can also cause allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and swelling, particularly in individuals with pre-existing sensitivities. Additionally, overconsumption of the plant's extracts can lead to cardiovascular problems, including high blood pressure and irregular heart rhythms. Prolonged exposure to Placus indicus can also result in liver damage and increased risk of bleeding disorders.
